questions.question: The method of preparing compost by using earthworm is called

options_data.explanation: The method of preparing compost by using earthworm is called vermiform composting, it helps in quick decomposing.

questions.question: If you dump kitchen waste in a pit, it may, after sometime:

options_data.explanation: If kitchen wastes are dumped into pits, then it may convert into compost. Kitchen wastes include biodegradable materials like the peel of the vegetables, fruits (highly nutritious) are very good manures. It contains proteins, lipids, and other organic materials that can support abundant populations of microorganisms.

questions.question: Which of the following activities does not reflect responsible behaviour with regard to waste disposal?

options_data.option: Goods carried in paper bags or cloth bags.
Waste collected in polythene bags for disposal.
options_data.explanation: This is because polythene bags are non-biodegradable. They do not decompose in nature on their own and are eaten up along with garbage food by cattle, thus causing harm to them.

options_data.option: Waste separated into those that degrade and those that do not.

questions.question:
Paheli gave the following ill effects of the practice of burning dried leaves and other plant parts

  1. Burning degrades the soil.
  2. Burning produces harmful gases/fumes.
  3. Precious raw materials to obtain manure at low cost are lost.
  4. A lot of heat is generated unnecessarily.

The correct reasons for why we should not burn leaves are

options_data.explanation:
1. Burning degrades the soil.
  2. Burning produces harmful gases/fumes.
  3. Precious raw materials to obtain manure a at low cost is lost.
  4. A lot of heat is generated unnecessarily.

questions.question:
A garbage collector separate items mentioned below in the garbage into red, green, and blue containers for their transfer to landfill, composting pit, and recycling unit respectively. Items:

  1. Plastic bags
  2. Newspaper and journals
  3. Screw and nuts
  4. Vegetable peels
  5. Metal chips
  6. Eggshells

Which item was transferred to which bin?

options_data.explanation:
• Green bins are used to collect biodegradable wastes like vegetable peels and eggshells.
  • Blue bin is used to collect the newspaper and journals.
  • Red bins are used to collect metal chips, plastics, screws, and nuts.

questions.question:
The steps required for conversion of kitchen garbage into manure are given below in a jumbled form.

  1. Put garbage in a pit
  2. Cover the bottom of the pit with sand
  3. Cover the pit loosely with a gunny bag or grass
  4. Add worms

Which of the following shows the correct sequence of the above steps?

options_data.explanation:
(ii) Cover the bottom of the pit with sand
(i) Put garbage in a pit
(iii) Cover the pit loosely with a gunny bag or grass
(iv) Add worms

questions.question:
Read the items mentioned in Columns I and II and fill in the related process in Column III.

Column I Column II Column III
(a) Organic waste Earthworms (i) ________
(b) Garbage Dig a pit and filled with garbage (ii) ________
(c) Old newspaper Paper bags (iii) ________

solution_data.solution:
1. Vermicomposting.
  2. Landfill.
  3. Recycling.

questions.question:
Correct the definitions of certain terms given below by changing only one word.

  1. Compost: Substances converted into manure for use in industries.
  2. Landfill: Garbage buried underwater in an area.
  3. Recycling: Reuse of unused material in the same or another form.

solution_data.solution:
The correct definitions are given below:

  1. Compost: Substances converted into manure for use in agricultural fields
  2. Landfill: Garbage buried under the soil in an area.
  3. Recycling: Reuse of used materials in the same or another form.

questions.question:
Provide a suitable term that expresses the meaning of the statement:
Greeting cards made from newspaper.

solution_data.solution: Recycling

questions.question:
Provide a suitable term that expresses the meaning of the statement:
Contents of the waste bins.

solution_data.solution: Garbage

questions.question:
Provide a suitable term that expresses the meaning of the statement:
Worms converting certain kinds of waste into manure.

solution_data.solution: Vermicomposting

questions.question:
Provide a suitable term that expresses the meaning of the statement:
An area where a lot of garbage is collected, spread out, and covered with soil.

solution_data.solution: Landfill

questions.question:
To what use can you put the following kinds of garbage and how?

  1. rotting smelly garbage
  2. dry leaves collected in a garbage
  3. old newspapers

solution_data.solution:
1. Rotting smelly garbage can be converted into compost or vermicompost. Compost can be made by hurrying the organic waste (vegetable peels, fruit peels, dry leaves, etc.) into a pit and covering it with soil. Vermicompost is made by using redworms-as follows:
    • Dig a pit or keep a wooden box in a suitable place in your garden.
    • Spread 1 or 2 cm thick layer of sand or a net or chicken mesh at the bottom of the pit or the box.
    • Spread the rotting smelly garbage along with dry leaves, dried stalks of plants, dried animal dung, etc. over the layer of sand.
    • Sprinkle some water over it to make the layer moist, and keep the layer loose.
    • Put redworms in the pit.
    • Cover the pit loosely with a gunny bag or an old sheet of cloth or a layer of grass.
    • The compost will be ready in about a month (3-4 weeks).
  2. Dry leaves collected in the garbage can be converted into compost or vermicompost. 
  3. Old newspapers can be used for covering the books, for making paper bags, for making paper pulp for use in handicrafts, and also for making recycled paper. For recycling, old newspapers can be given to the kabadiwala (junk dealer).

questions.question: Paheli was writing a letter to her friend. She crumpled and threw the first draft of her letter on the floor as it had become untidy. Similarly, she crumpled and threw 6 more papers on the ground. In the end, she picked them up and put them in a polythene bag, and threw them on the road outside her house. Do you think Paheli’s actions were responsible? What would you have done if you were in her place?

solution_data.solution: Paheli's action shows irresponsible behavior. By doing so, she is adding garbage to the environment. Instead of throwing the used papers, she can use them in different ways. Moreover, by throwing polythene bags on the road, she is adding non-biodegradable waste to the environment. If I were, at her place, then I would have either used the waste paper for doing rough work or converted the waste paper into paper pulp to make handicraft items or recycled paper.

questions.question:
Read the poem written below and then answer the questions from the information gathered from the book or elsewhere
Blue and Green
Two bins, you mean?
Yes, they are there
to throw your waste.
But not in a hurry
Nor in a haste.
Select from waste, sieve if seems muddy
Separate all items and when they are ready
Place in a blue bin, or one that is green
For a voyage to the landfill, or for composting.

  1. Name the two kinds of waste that need to be separated from each other in two different waste bins.
  2. Name two items of waste each that need to be sent to a
    1. landfill,
    2. for composting.

solution_data.solution:
1. The two kinds of waste that need to be separated from each other in two different waste are named biodegradable waste and non-biodegradable waste. Two different colored waste bins, green and blue can be used to separate garbage. The green bin would contain biodegradable waste that can be converted into compost by bacteria or earthworms. The blue bin would contain dry recyclable waste. The waste in the green bin is used for composting while that in a blue bin is used for landfills.
    1. The two items that can be sent to the landfill are empty glass bottles and plastic plates.
    2. Kitchen wastes and animal dung can be sent for composting.

questions.question:
Beera, a farmer would clear his field every day, and burn dry leaves fallen on the ground. After some time he found that those living in huts near his field were suffering from cough and breathing problems.

  1. Can you explain why?
  2. Also, suggest an environment-friendly way to dispose of the dry leaves.

solution_data.solution:
1. Burning of dry leaves produces harmful fumes and gases which cause cough, breathing problems, etc. People living in huts near Beera's field suffered from these problems as they inhaled the harmful fumes and gases released by the burning of dry leaves.
  2. Dry leaves can be put in a pit for composting. Composting is a method of preparing manure. It is an environment-friendly way to dispose of dry leaves. The manure produced from the dry leaves can be used by farmers in the agriculture field for the enrichment of the soil.

questions.question:
The pie charts A and B showed in Fig. are based on waste segregation method adopted by two families X and Y.

Which of the two families X or Y do you think is more environmentally conscious and why?

solution_data.solution: Family X is more environmentally conscious because it segregated biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances separately, and on the basis of the further use of the waste as for composting, recycling or landfill. It disposed kitchen waste (biodegradable) and polythene bags (non-biodegradable) separately unlike family Y who disposed kitchen waste and polythene bags together as one waste.

questions.question: Write three sentences on what comes to your mind when you chance to see A rag picker.

solution_data.solution:
1. A rag picker represents poor people or the poverty around us.
  2. He removes the garbage generated by us by sorting out the recyclable waste from non-recyclable waste.
  3. He is exposing himself to the harmful substances generated by solid waste.

questions.question: Write three sentences on what comes to your mind when you chance to see a cow eating a polythene bag.

solution_data.solution:
1. A cow eating a polythene bag shows the irresponsible behavior of humans towards the disposal of garbage in the form of a polythene bag.
  2. A cow eating polythene is exposing itself to hazardous material generating from the garbage.
  3. There is a possibility of polythene bag choking in the gut of an animal which can lead to its death.

questions.question:
Write three sentences on what comes to your mind when you chance to see:
A foul odour emanating from garbage at the entrance of your house.

solution_data.solution:
1. Foul odour emanating from garbage at the entrance of our house represents the production of a large quantity of waste.
  2. It shows our irresponsibility towards garbage disposal, as we are expecting others to clear the waste generated by us.
  3. The foul garbage shows unhealthy surroundings with an unpleasant sight which would lead to the spread of diseases. It shows the irresponsible behaviour of the municipality concerned as they should get the garbage cleared from the city.

questions.question: Beautiful hand-crafted articles like boxes and toys are made of paper pulp in our country. Can you explain how paper pulp which is made from paper can be used to make hard boxes and other articles?

solution_data.solution: The paper pulp obtained from the paper is mixed with glue and saw-dust. Glue and saw-dust make the paper hard. For making hard boxes, toys, etc. few coats of this hard pulp are applied over the framework. It is then allowed to dried up. Once the pulp is dried up, hard boxes and toys can further be processed.

questions.question: Recently, a ban on plastic bags has been imposed in many places? Is the ban justified? Give reasons in three sentences.

solution_data.solution:
Yes, a ban on plastic bags is justified because:

  1. Plastic gives out harmful gases on heating or burning. These gases may cause pollution and many health problems including cancer in humans.
  2. People fill garbage in plastic bags and throw them away on roads and other places like garbage dumps. When stray animals look for food in these bags, they end up swallowing the bags too. This results in their death due to choking.
  3. Plastic bags thrown away carelessly on roads and other places get into drains and the sewage system. As a result, drains get choked, and the water spills on the roads. During heavy rains, it might even create a flood-like situation.

questions.question: Why should we not burn plastic items?

solution_data.solution:
We should not burn plastic items because:

  1. Plastic is a non-biodegradable substance, so it cannot be easily burnt.
  2. On burning, harmful gases and fumes are liberated out which are hazardous to the health of humans and animals and also to the environment.
  3. The leftover burnt pieces of plastic can be eaten by animals which may lead to their death by choking them.
  4. The ashes left after the burning of plastic are toxic in nature and thus, harm the environment.
  5. Plastic is not environment-friendly. Its burning causes soil pollution and air pollution.

questions.question: What happens when the cooking medium is made to flow down a drain?

solution_data.solution: When a cooking medium is made to flow down a drain, it may choke the pipes causing drainage problems and water spills. If the cooking medium reaches the soil, it will clog the pores in the soil causing aeration problems that result in the death of useful microbes present in the soil, decrease in soil fertility, and soil pollution.

questions.question: What happens when Insecticides, motor oil, paints are poured down the drain?

solution_data.solution: When insecticides, motor oil, and paints are poured down the drain, they may choke the pipes causing drainage problems. On reaching the agricultural field; they clog the pores of soil leading to aeration problem. If the discharge reaches the water bodies, it pollutes the water and may cause various harmful diseases in aquatic animals.

questions.question: What happens when Tea leaves, cotton swabs, and old soft toys are thrown into the drain?

solution_data.solution: If tea leaves, cotton swabs and old soft toys are thrown into the drain, it can choke the drains and block the water supply of the area.

questions.question: Why should we prefer to use paper bags rather than polythene bags?

solution_data.solution: We should prefer using paper bags rather than polythene bags as paper can be decomposed of easily. This reduce the generation of plastic garbage.

questions.question: Who, out of the following should properly dispose of the garbage – father, mother, elder brother, younger sister?

solution_data.solution: Every member of society or family is responsible to dispose of the garbage properly.

questions.question: Which one out of beetles, roundworm, and earthworm are used for vermicomposting and why?

solution_data.solution: Earthworms are the best organisms to be used for vermicomposting as they convert the waste from plants, animals, or their products into compost.
